node.title: Slums
node.description: You stand in the rain-slicked alleys of the Slums, neon lights flickering above. This is where all infiltrations begin. The Night Market lies ahead, protected by encrypted terminals.

node.title: Night Market
node.description: The Night Market buzzes with illicit data trades and black-market tech. Street vendors hawk stolen credentials while holographic ads flicker overhead. A terminal blocks the path to the Security Gate.

node.title: Security Gate
node.description: Massive corporate firewalls manifest as towering gates of light. Security drones patrol the perimeter. Beyond lies the DATACORE—the ultimate prize. But first, you must crack the gate's encryption.

node.title: Data Core
node.description: The DATACORE pulses with raw information—corporate secrets, financial records, backdoors to the entire network. One final puzzle stands between you and total access. The alarm is screaming. Make it count.

puzzle.prompt: This area is not safe. You need to quickly get out of here. There is a beaten down bicycle nearby, but it has a 4-digit lockpad.
puzzle.clue: The bicycle is tied to a fence. The house has a large Dinamo logo painted on the wall. The owner must be a fan.
The code is currently set to 0875, maybe the owner scrambled it poorely.
There is a sticker on the bicycle. It has a bulldog head with the letters BBB underneath. Could this mean something?

puzzle.prompt: The street vendor's terminal flickers with a cracked login screen. Enter the access code.
puzzle.clue: You spot graffiti on the wall: "Remember 4/20"
The terminal has worn keys: 0, 2, 4. Someone uses this code frequently.
It's a 4-digit code. The pattern suggests a date format: MMDD

puzzle.prompt: The Security Gate demands authentication. A numeric keypad awaits your input.
puzzle.clue: Scrawled on a nearby datapad: "Elite access only"
The word "LEET" is spray-painted on the gate. Hacker slang from the old net.

puzzle.prompt: The DATACORE's final lock pulses red. One last code stands between you and victory.
puzzle.clue: A meme flashes on the screen: "IT'S OVER 9000!"
The number 9000 appears, but it's crossed out. Someone wrote "+1" next to it.
